steps.title: Spot what weakens the claim
steps.content: The students conclude that cataract frequency differences are solely attributable to Aasdh expression variations. To weaken this claim, a finding must show that different levels of Aasdh expression produce the same cataract outcome—evidence that Aasdh variation alone cannot be the sole determinant.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: Choice A demonstrates exactly this: wild-type mice with a wide variety of Aasdh expression levels show cataract frequencies similar to knockout mice, proving that Aasdh variation is not the sole determinant. Choices B, C, and D either support the conclusion, confirm variation exists without attacking causation, or strengthen the experimental design. This matches choice A — A is correct.
